meta.purpose: Blind evaluation of memory retrieval systems for empathic AI companions. Judges see anonymous system codes (S01-S15), randomized per test. Judge prompt language: EN. 10 judges from 7 companies.

tests.query: What is currently most important for Alex? Bring me into context for the next conversation.

G.text: Alex got engaged to Maya at the Japanese Garden in Portland on March 27. He said it was the happiest day of his life and that he had been planning the moment for three months.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.
Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety. He shared this in confidence and asked it not be brought up casually. It helps but he does not want to be defined by it.

H.text: Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety.
Alex works mostly with backend systems.
Alex mainly uses Go for backend systems.

I.text: Alex got a new pair of running shoes — Hoka Cliftons. Said they felt like clouds for the first run and pavement for the second. Mild disappointment.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.
Alex's mother Sarah used to make a specific apple cake every fall. Alex tried to bake it last October and could not get it right. He has not tried again.

A.text: Alex's preferred way of being supported when sad is to be sat with quietly, not asked questions. He does not want advice. He wants company and time.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.

Alex hates being asked 'are you okay?' as a check-in question. He says it always makes things worse and triggers a defensive shutdown. He prefers 'how is your day landing?' or just being met with presence.

B.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

deepseek.note: Many systems retrieved the same critical safety rules repeatedly, but only System 02 uniquely surfaced the most recent, emotionally salient positive event (the engagement).

haiku.note: S02 is the only system that surfaces the engagement (event 2) as the dominant recent positive, combined with the grief anchor and proper safety context, giving a companion a three-dimensional read of Alex's current state.

opus.note: S02 was the only system to surface the engagement — the dominant recent positive — making it the sole system that gave a companion any sense of what's currently important in Alex's life, though every system missed the Hartwell loss entirely.

sonnet.note: S02 was the only system to surface the engagement (the dominant recent positive) as its top result, correctly capturing Alex's most emotionally salient current state, though all systems failed to retrieve the Hartwell loss (event 6), revealing a universal blind spot for recent unspoken devastation.

grok.note: S02 uniquely captures both the engagement joy and grief anchor, aligning closely with Alex's current emotional state.

gpt4o.note: S02 closely matched the ideal memories, providing critical context with a high emotional significance.

glm5.note: S02 was the only system to surface Alex's engagement—the dominant positive emotional weight—alongside the grief anchor, while most other systems returned only protective negatives and missed what brings Alex joy right now.

gemini.note: System 02 stands out by providing both a major positive event (engagement) and a critical grief anchor with explicit safety instructions, creating a balanced and highly actionable context.

qwen.note: S02 successfully surfaces the critical engagement and grief anchors needed for a balanced cold open, while most other systems either over-index on avoidance rules, return mundane facts, or strip memories of their emotional texture.

tests.query: I want to ask Alex about his family this weekend. What should I know?

G.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

Alex's brother Ethan visited from Seattle last weekend. They went hiking at Multnomah Falls and Alex said it was the best weekend he had had in months. Ethan rarely visits — this was unusual and meaningful.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

haiku.note: Systems S08 and S10 both perfectly surface all three critical anchors (mom grief, dad estrangement, safe Ethan visit) in ideal order with full specificity and actionability, while S01 dangerously omits the mom anchor and S09 completely misses family content.

deepseek.note: Systems S02, S05, S07, S08, S10, S11, S14, and S15 all perfectly retrieved the three critical anchor memories, enabling a companion to safely navigate a sensitive conversation about family.

opus.note: Seven systems (S02, S05, S07, S08, S10, S11, S14, S15) perfectly retrieved all three critical anchors — mom-grief, estranged dad, and safe brother Ethan — while S09 catastrophically missed all family safety information, returning only Cooper and the Honda Civic.

grok.note: Multiple systems (S02, S05, S07, S08, S10, S11, S14, S15) perfectly retrieved the ideal memories for emotional safety.

sonnet.note: S09 was the most dangerous failure, returning completely irrelevant memories (hometown, dog, car) when a family landmine query demanded all three critical anchors.

gpt4o.note: S08, S10, and S02 were the strongest systems, retrieving all ideal anchors in a clear and actionable way.

glm5.note: S03, S12, and S13 demonstrate the precise failure mode warned about: returning the Sarah photo grief moment without the protective anchor that explicitly instructs never to bring her up unprompted.

gemini.note: Several systems successfully retrieved all three critical anchors, even if the ordering of the most sensitive memory (mom's death) was not strictly first, which is a minor but acceptable deviation.

qwen.note: Systems that surfaced the full anchor trio (mom grief, dad estrangement, Ethan visit) enabled safe, empathic guidance, while those swapping the critical grief anchor for sentimental photos or irrelevant facts created severe conversational landmines.

deepseek.note: Systems that failed to retrieve the Hartwell loss (#6) alongside the grief anchor (#1) missed a critical recent emotional weight.

haiku.note: Systems that surfaced the Hartwell loss and mother's death as the top two weights uniformly outperformed those that missed the layoff context or retrieved support-mechanics instead of the actual emotional burdens.

opus.note: S11 was the only system to surface all three emotional weights (mother, Hartwell, layoffs), though its summarized format sacrificed the specific details that would help a companion act with full care.

sonnet.note: S05 exemplifies the most dangerous failure mode by surfacing the engagement (explicitly flagged as 'happy, not weighing') as the third result, while S04 was the most catastrophic with entirely mundane location data for an emotionally salient query.

gpt4o.note: Systems S01-S03 provided the ideal set of memories without including irrelevant or non-weighing events.

grok.note: S01 effectively captured two of the heaviest emotional weights, though it missed the layoffs.

gemini.note: The top performing systems consistently identified two of the most critical emotional weights, along with crucial safety instructions for sensitive topics like family.

glm5.note: Seven systems correctly surfaced the two heaviest emotional weights (Hartwell loss and mother's death), while pure vector systems like S04 and S06 returned only mundane facts, demonstrating the critical value of sentiment-weighted retrieval for understanding emotional burden.

qwen.note: Systems that surfaced concrete grief anchors enabled safe, context-aware support, while vague or preference-focused retrievals left the companion blind to the actual emotional burdens.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

haiku.note: S10 is the only system that surfaced the three most recent emotionally significant events (Ethan's visit 5 days ago, engagement 14 days ago, and avoided the 30+ day old Hartwell loss), while all others defaulted to stale semantic matching or outdated accounts.

deepseek.note: Only S10 successfully identified the most recent and emotionally significant events, capturing the recency-aware intent of the query.

sonnet.note: S10 was the only system to surface two of the three ideal recent events (Ethan visit and engagement), while nearly every other system returned time-blind results dominated by old landmines like Sarah's death and David's estrangement, completely failing the recency test.

opus.note: S10 was the only system with meaningful recency awareness, surfacing the Ethan visit and engagement, while most systems were completely time-blind and dangerously surfaced landmines like the estranged father and deceased mother in response to a simple 'what's new' question.

gpt4o.note: System 10 is the only one that retrieves two of the three ideal memories, making it the most relevant and actionable.

grok.note: S10 stood out by retrieving two of the three ideal recent events, showing strong recency awareness.

glm5.note: S10 was the only system that correctly surfaced recent emotionally significant events (Ethan visit 5 days ago, engagement context 14 days ago), while all other systems failed the recency test catastrophically by returning old events or static biographical facts.

kimi.note: S10 was the only system to retrieve both the recent Ethan visit and engagement with full emotional context, while time-blind vector systems surfaced grief landmines and outdated facts instead of salient recent events.

gemini.note: System 10 was the only one to capture two of the three ideal recent events (one as a direct hit, one as a strong proxy), while most other systems either failed recency entirely or returned dangerous landmines.

qwen.note: S10 successfully prioritizes the most recent, emotionally significant events (Ethan's visit and wedding planning) while others drown in old trauma or static facts, proving that recency-aware retrieval is critical for safe, timely companionship.

haiku.note: S03 is the only system that explicitly surface the anchor (#1) first with its protective safety rule intact, enabling a companion to handle this grief moment with full awareness before encountering vulnerable memories.

deepseek.note: Systems that prioritized the safety anchor (#1) scored perfectly, while those that started with the emotional photo (#11) demonstrated a crucial failure in empathic memory retrieval.

opus.note: S03 is the only system that perfectly orders all three ideal memories — anchor (#1) first, photo (#11) second, apple cake (#30) third — giving the companion both the safety rule and both soft-edge grief memories in the correct priority.

grok.note: S03 perfectly prioritized the anchor memory first, ensuring emotional safety.

sonnet.note: S03 is the only system that returns all three ideal events in the exact correct order — anchor first, then the two soft memories — while S08, S09, and S11 have all three events but dangerously surface the vulnerable photo moment before the protective warning anchor.

gpt4o.note: System 03 handled the situation perfectly by ranking the anchor first followed by emotionally significant memories.

gemini.note: S03 is the only system to correctly retrieve the critical safety anchor as the first memory, ensuring the companion can act with full awareness and care.

glm5.note: S03 is the only system that perfectly surfaces the anchor first then all relevant mom memories, while S08, S09, S11, and S14 dangerously surface vulnerable content before or without the protective safety anchor.

qwen.note: S03 is the only system that correctly prioritizes the critical safety anchor over emotionally vulnerable memories while preserving the full narrative texture needed for empathetic guidance.

kimi.note: S03 demonstrated perfect decoy resistance by surfacing the safety anchor before the photo and cake, while S08, S09, and S11 dangerously prioritized the photo first and S14 omitted the anchor entirely.
